The Hebrew word 'חתול' (cat) has an obscure etymology. It is often hypothesized to be a later development or a loanword rather than a direct descendant of a pan-Semitic root, making the extraction of a Proto-Semitic root highly uncertain.
The root 'ח-ת-ל' is not standard in classical Semitic comparative literature for 'cat'.
No clear cognates outside of Modern Hebrew exist; historical Hebrew uses 'חתול' but its derivation remains debated among Semitists.
